fields.description:
## Why

The T2 semantic-resolution tier (epic `850f`) was deferred out of the v1 code-grounding floor as a
placeholder with three candidate backends and unresolved security/determinism tradeoffs. Before any
code lands, the selection and the confirm-only policy must be recorded as a durable decision, and the
IaC grounding lane (a different domain) must be split out so it is not silently folded into this epic.
This story produces that record; S2/S3 implement against it.

## What

- Add an ADR at `docs/adr/00NN-code-grounding-t2-semantic-resolution.md` (next free ADR number) that
  records:
  - **Decision:** v1 T2 backend = one-shot compiler/checker-CLI diagnostics, first language Python via
    `pyright --outputjson`. LSP-client-library and containerized/hermetic tiers are **deferred** (with
    their cost/security/determinism tradeoffs stated: LSP multiplies per-language server/runtime/config
    surface; containerized needs an egress-locked single-tenant microVM sandbox because building
    untrusted client repos is arbitrary code execution).
  - **Confirm-only mapping:** a T2 `refuted` is emitted only when the checker ran to completion (file has no
    import-resolution diagnostics = environment built) and there is no diagnostic at the reference;
    every other case (tool-absent / unsupported-lang / no-file / parse-error / timeout / crash /
    env-not-built / diagnostic-present) is `abstain` with a closed structured reason. T2 never asserts
    an absence and never sits on a deterministic blocking path.
  - **Minimal-seam decision (Rule-of-Three):** with one concrete backend, no `T2Backend` Protocol +
    plugin registry — S2 ships a single `refute_semantic()` dispatch function selected by config. A
    future backend adds a name + a branch.
  - **Consumers unchanged:** T2 records reuse the existing three-valued contract and closed
    vocabularies (`TIER_T2` already exists), so `5fd2`/`9da1` consumers need no edit.
- Reference the ADR from `docs/grounding.md` (a one-line pointer under the tiers section) so the design
  is discoverable from the grounding doc.
- Create a **sibling epic** for the IaC grounding lane (`terraform validate` = IaC T1, Registry
  provider/module existence = IaC T0, `terraform plan` = IaC T2) and `link <iac-epic> 850f relates_to`
  so the split is recorded in the graph.

## Scope

In scope: the ADR document, the `docs/grounding.md` pointer, and the sibling IaC epic + link. Out of
scope: any `src/` change (that is S2/S3) — this is a design/doc-only story.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/adr/0030-code-grounding-t2-semantic-resolution.md` exists and follows the repo's ADR
      format (Status/Date/Context/Decision/Consequences headings, matching a sibling ADR's structure).
- [ ] The ADR records the backend-selection decision (pyright compiler-CLI diagnostics for v1).
- [ ] The ADR records the confirm-only mapping (`refuted` only on a completed run with no
      import-resolution diagnostics and no diagnostic at the reference; every other case `abstain`).
- [ ] The ADR records the minimal-seam (no-registry) rationale and the LSP/containerized deferral with
      their stated tradeoffs.
- [ ] `docs/grounding.md` links to the new ADR from its tiers/T2 section.
- [ ] A sibling epic for the IaC grounding lane exists, linked `relates_to` epic `850f`, with a
      description scoping the IaC T0/T1/T2 tiers.
- [ ] `make lint` exits 0.
- [ ] The change touches only files under `docs/` (no `src/` edits).
